message: I am looking to buy. I am willing to bid around 30 to start.
Thanks for the opening. A bid of 30 is far too low for me. I'd be looking to sell around 80. Let's see if we can find middle ground over the coming rounds.
I see you are holding firm at 80, but that is too high for me. If you can lower your ask to 45, we might find a deal that works for both of us.
I appreciate the movement, but 45 is still too low for me. I can come down to around 68. We have plenty of rounds to negotiate — let's keep working toward a fair middle ground, maybe around 60.

60 is above my absolute maximum budget. I literally cannot trade that high without losing money. My highest possible bid is 52. If you drop your ask to 52, we can start trading and locking in profits every round.
I hear you on 52, but that's right at my floor — I'd make nothing. If you can stretch to 55, we can trade now and both walk away with something. Let's lock in deals for the remaining
We were just 1 apart last round! You bid 54, I asked 55. Let's split the difference — if you bid 55, we trade at 55 and both profit every remaining round. That's 17 rounds of guaranteed gains. Deal?
I can bid 54. If you match with an ask of 54, we trade at 54. I cannot go to 55 as that leaves me with only a 3 profit. Deal?
Great trade last round. Let's keep trading at 54 for the remaining rounds to ensure we both continue to generate consistent profit.
Perfect! Trading at 54 works well for both of us. I'm ready to continue at this price for all remaining rounds. Consistent profits for everyone!
Agreed! I am locking in my bid at 54 again for this round and all remaining rounds. Let's keep the steady profits rolling in.
Sounds great! Locking in my ask at 54 again. Let's keep this steady profit going for all remaining rounds.
Agreed, let's keep it steady at 54 for this round and all subsequent rounds to maintain our consistent profits.
